name: Local Flood (TCP)
notes: Detects when a single local host sends a large number of packets (greater than 1000pps) to an internet destination over a small period of time.  The packet rate in this rule can be adjusted as needed to reflect the network.
conditions_text: when the flow bias is any of the following outbound
when the source packet rate is greater than 1000 packets/second
when the IP protocol is one of the following TCP
when the source packets is greater than 60000
TD_list: APPLY Local Flood (TCP) on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the flow bias is any of the following outbound
AND when the source packet rate is greater than 1000 packets/second
AND when the IP protocol is one of the following TCP
AND when the source packets is greater than 60000
RED_list: Event description: Single local host sending a large number of packets (greater than 1000pps) to an internet destination over a small period of time. This can indicate an attack or a service that has become unresponsive.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should not contribute to the naming of the associated offense(s)
